main.purpose: Primary response model — quality and identity fidelity matter most. Used for all user-facing replies across CLI, Web, and Telegram. Prices in BRL are approximate and used by the Context Rail to surface session cost; edit when provider rates shift.

reception.purpose: Classification of incoming messages across three independent axes — persona, organization, journey. Runs before every response to route the message. Gemini 2.5 Flash with reasoning=minimal was chosen after a three-model eval (Haiku 4.5, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Gemini 2.5 Pro) showed Flash matching Haiku's accuracy (9/11 on scope-routing probes) at roughly 3× lower per-token cost, with comparable latency. Gemini 2.5 Pro performed better in theory but was blocked by a pi-ai parsing issue via OpenRouter. When Code Assist for Individuals OAuth lands, this role migrates to provider='google-gemini-cli' and the cost drops to zero under the free tier. Admins can always override via /admin/models; reception falls back silently on any failure. Prices in BRL are approximate.

title.purpose: Background summarization — generates a 3-6 word title for a session when the user ends it via 'Begin again'. Fire-and-forget: failure leaves the session untitled and the episodic browse falls back to 'Untitled conversation'. Speed and cost matter; semantic nuance is less critical than a decent-enough label.
